• Main Page
  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples

Utility Routines (util.h)
[C Library]

Detailed Description

C library extensions that provide a variety of utility operations.

To include these routines, do:

 #include <util.h>

Defines

#define likely(x)   __builtin_expect((x) != 0, 1)
 Produce (x != 0) but hint to the compiler it is likely to be 1.
#define unlikely(x)   __builtin_expect((x) != 0, 0)
 Produce (x != 0) but hint to the compiler it is likely to be 0.
#define min(a, b)
 Find the minimum of two values.
#define max(a, b)
 Find the maximum of two values.
#define abs(a)
 Find the absolute value of a value.
#define swap(a, b)
 Swap two values.
#define cpu_to_le16(x)   (x)
 Convert a 2-byte value to little-endian format.
#define cpu_to_le32(x)   (x)
 Convert a 4-byte value to little-endian format.
#define cpu_to_le64(x)   (x)
 Convert a 8-byte value to little-endian format.
#define cpu_to_be16(x)   (__builtin_bswap32(x) >> 16)
 Convert a 2-byte value to big-endian format.
#define cpu_to_be32(x)   __builtin_bswap32(x)
 Convert a 4-byte value to big-endian format.
#define cpu_to_be64(x)   __builtin_bswap64(x)
 Convert a 8-byte value to big-endian format.
#define le16_to_cpu   cpu_to_le16
 Convert a 2-byte value from little-endian format.
#define le32_to_cpu   cpu_to_le32
 Convert a 4-byte value from little-endian format.
#define le64_to_cpu   cpu_to_le64
 Convert a 8-byte value from little-endian format.
#define be16_to_cpu   cpu_to_be16
 Convert a 2-byte value from big-endian format.
#define be32_to_cpu   cpu_to_be32
 Convert a 4-byte value from big-endian format.
#define be64_to_cpu   cpu_to_be64
 Convert a 8-byte value from big-endian format.

Functions

void panic (const char *fmt,...)
 Panic the system.
void panic_start (const char *fmt,...)
 Start to panic the system; used when you need multi-stage panic output, like a panic message followed by a register dump.
void panic_end (void)
 Finish panicking the system.
void exit (int status)
 Exit the program.
void abort (void)
 Panics with an abort message.
int str2l (const char *nptr, char **endptr, int base, long *val_p)
 Convert a string to an integer, a la strtol().
int str2ll (const char *nptr, char **endptr, int base, long long *val_p)
 Convert a string to a long long integer, a la strtoll().

Define Documentation

#define likely ( x   )     __builtin_expect((x) != 0, 1)

Produce (x != 0) but hint to the compiler it is likely to be 1.

#define unlikely ( x   )     __builtin_expect((x) != 0, 0)

Produce (x != 0) but hint to the compiler it is likely to be 0.

#define min ( a,
b   ) 

Value:

({ \
    typeof (a) _a = (a); \
    typeof (b) _b = (b); \
    _a < _b ? _a : _b; \
  })
Find the minimum of two values.

Parameters:
a First value.
b Second value.
Returns:
Minimum of a and b.

#define max ( a,
b   ) 

Value:

({ \
    typeof (a) _a = (a); \
    typeof (b) _b = (b); \
    _a > _b ? _a : _b; \
  })
Find the maximum of two values.

Parameters:
a First value.
b Second value.
Returns:
Maximum of a and b.

#define abs ( a   ) 

Value:

({ \
    typeof (a) _a = (a); \
    _a >= 0 ? _a : -_a; \
  })
Find the absolute value of a value.

Parameters:
a Value.
Returns:
a if a >= 0, else -a.

#define swap ( a,
b   ) 

Value:

({ \
    typeof (a) _tmp = (a); \
    (a) = (b); \
    (b) = _tmp; \
  })
Swap two values.

Parameters:
a Value.
b Another value.

#define cpu_to_le16 ( x   )     (x)

Convert a 2-byte value to little-endian format.

#define cpu_to_le32 ( x   )     (x)

Convert a 4-byte value to little-endian format.

#define cpu_to_le64 ( x   )     (x)

Convert a 8-byte value to little-endian format.

#define cpu_to_be16 ( x   )     (__builtin_bswap32(x) >> 16)

Convert a 2-byte value to big-endian format.

#define cpu_to_be32 ( x   )     __builtin_bswap32(x)

Convert a 4-byte value to big-endian format.

#define cpu_to_be64 ( x   )     __builtin_bswap64(x)

Convert a 8-byte value to big-endian format.

#define le16_to_cpu   cpu_to_le16

Convert a 2-byte value from little-endian format.

#define le32_to_cpu   cpu_to_le32

Convert a 4-byte value from little-endian format.

#define le64_to_cpu   cpu_to_le64

Convert a 8-byte value from little-endian format.

#define be16_to_cpu   cpu_to_be16

Convert a 2-byte value from big-endian format.

#define be32_to_cpu   cpu_to_be32

Convert a 4-byte value from big-endian format.

#define be64_to_cpu   cpu_to_be64

Convert a 8-byte value from big-endian format.

Function Documentation

void panic ( const char *  fmt,
  ... 
)

Panic the system.

Parameters:
fmt printf-style format (followed by printf-style arguments).

void panic_start ( const char *  fmt,
  ... 
)

Start to panic the system; used when you need multi-stage panic output, like a panic message followed by a register dump.

This routine prints the initial panic message, but does not actually halt; the caller is expected to call panic_end() when any additional output is finished.

Parameters:
fmt printf-style format (followed by printf-style arguments).

void panic_end ( void   ) 

Finish panicking the system.

void exit ( int  status  ) 

Exit the program.

Parameters:
status Exit status.
Examples:
bme/client_server/bme_server.c.

void abort ( void   ) 

Panics with an abort message.

Needed for libgcc.a.

int str2l ( const char *  nptr,
char **  endptr,
int  base,
long *  val_p 
)

Convert a string to an integer, a la strtol().

We return an error status (or zero for success), and the actual integer is returned through the required val_p pointer.

int str2ll ( const char *  nptr,
char **  endptr,
int  base,
long long *  val_p 
)

Convert a string to a long long integer, a la strtoll().

We return an error status (or zero for success), and the actual integer is returned through the required val_p pointer.
